full_text:
# **CIN2 Project - t-CyCIF Analysis - next steps**

The purpose of this document is to serve as a guide for the next steps
of t-CyCIF analysis. The images used are examples from the publication
by Perez-Villatoro et al. 2026, and their purpose is to illustrate the
desired results and how they should be depicted.

1. **[Sample pooling]{.underline}\
 > **

 - Pool the samples from the same patient into a single, combined
 > sample

2. **[Descriptive Analysis\
 > ]{.underline}**

 - **Cell Counts & Relative Proportion of Cells\
 > **

 - Conduct **cell count analysis** and determine the **relative
 > proportions of different cell types** in the samples.

 - all cell types (image c)

 - epithelia (image g)

 - immune cells (image
 > g)![](media/image5.jpg){width="5.802083333333333in"
 > height="2.9223687664041993in"}![](media/image7.jpg){width="5.364583333333333in"
 > height="2.8598687664041993in"}

 - **Functional markers** in **different cell groups** (image
 > l)

 - immune cells

 - epithelial cells

![](media/image6.jpg){width="5.703125546806649in"
height="3.3726126421697287in"}

- **E4 status**

 - [**[E4
 > status]{.underline}**](https://docs.google.com/spreadsheets/d/1TqXvCewhG4N2CCW5C_-x1UwHRibiGj97XCBL_pPJL6g/edit?usp=sharing)

- **Group Classification\
 > **

 - Categorize samples into three distinct groups based on
 > regression status:

d/1TqXvCewhG4N2CCW5C_-x1UwHRibiGj97XCBL_pPJL6g/edit?usp=sharing)

- **Group Classification\
 > **

 - Categorize samples into three distinct groups based on
 > regression status:

 - Non-regressors (non-reg + ca progressors)

 - Quick regressors

 - Regressors

- **Comparative Analysis\
 > **

 - Perform the following pairwise comparisons:

 - All regressors vs. non-regressors

 - Quick regressors vs. all others

3. **[Recurrent Cellular Neighborhood Analysis (RCNs)]{.underline}\
 > **

 - recurrent cellular neighborhood analysis to study the spatial
 > organization and interactions between cell populations.

 - during this step is important to look at the images if we these
 > RCNs are true![](media/image4.png){width="5.189194006999125in"
 > height="5.265365266841645in"}

 - Interpretation of the RCN groups (image e):

 - Examples of spatial domains that we would like to find

 a. Lesion-stroma interphase

 b. lesion

 c. normal epithelia

 d. stroma

 e. immune

 f. glandular (p63 negative epithelia)

> ![](media/image3.png){width="2.682292213473316in"
> height="3.2225415573053366in"}

- **Comparative RCN Analysis (image k)\
 > **

 - Compare the following groups:

 - Regressors vs. non-regressors

 - Regressors vs. quick
 > regressors![](media/image2.png){width="3.092622484689414in"
 > height="4.234375546806649in"}

> 4\. **[Future Analysis with SpaceStat]{.underline}\
> **

- Regressors vs. quick
 > regressors![](media/image2.png){width="3.092622484689414in"
 > height="4.234375546806649in"}

> 4\. **[Future Analysis with SpaceStat]{.underline}\
> **

- spatial analysis using SpaceStat to explore the arrangement of T
 > cells in the stroma, TLSs?

> 5\. Cancer samples ??
